EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unusual strong surface high pressure, combined with a strong low pressure system, to produce a very tight pressure gradient across the area. As a result, strong low level winds dominated the region from February 11 through February 13, 2018. A wind advisory was issued for all Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at TJBQ airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers with thunderstorms developed in the afternoon hours due to the combination of a  strong upper level trough and low level moisture. The activity developed across the Cordillera Central of Puerto Rico and moved southwestward along the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pea size hail was reported around 2 PM AST along road PR-132. Public and social media posts confirmed additional small hail reports.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep and strong surface low pressure across the western north Atlantic, produced large long period north to northwest swells. These swells were up to 20 feet high in some areas with breaking wave between 25 to 30 feet. ||During the early morning hours of Sunday March 3rd, the buoy 41043 began to report a sharp increase on the NNW swell height and period. Later that day the near shore Atlantic and Rincon buoys also reported a sharp increase. Reports of large swell affecting the northern and western coast of Puerto Rico began on the afternoon hours. The U.S. Coast Guard rescued 3 surfers in the town of Aguadilla as they could not swim to the shore due to large breakers. Also several roads had to be closed as large swells began to flood them. The most significant damage occurred during late evening and midnight on Sunday when high tide aggravated the flooding process along the west and northern coast of Puerto Rico where several roads were affected by flooding waters and debris. In addition, concrete structures along the coast were affected by the swell energy. Most of the coasts of Puerto Rico and USVI remained highly vulnerable after the impacts of hurricanes Irma and Maria.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several cars and people were trapped due to flooding caused by the swell action in Pi��ones area. Road 187 was closed due to flooding and large amount of debris affecting the road. Significant erosion occurred along the coast, mainly on Parcelas Suarez.

Many schools and government offices were closed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low level moisture combined with daytime heating and orographic effects to produce scattered to locally numerous showers across the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico. Heavy rainfall led to rapid rises in small stream and rivers across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 56 year old man was dragged by a surge of water on the Guatemala river. PREMA search and rescue team found the body down river.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong surface high pressure across the central Atlantic produced hazardous seas between 5 to 7 feet and winds up to 20 knots. This created dangerous rip currents along the north coast of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A person was trapped by rip currents in the vicinity of Ocean Park in Condado sector in San Juan. The person was rescued successfully without no major issues.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Plenty of low-level moisture combined with diurnal heating and local effects to result in the development of slow moving showers, mainly over interior PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding and mudslides prompted the closure of road PR-523 at KM 1 in Barrio Arenas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Residual moisture from a tropical wave combined with diurnal heating and local effects to result in the development of showers and thunderstorms across northwest PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains created urban and small stream flooding on Road 115 KM 17.6 in Barrio Pueblo and in Barrio Calbache KM 0.1.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture associated with a tropical wave combined with diurnal heating and local effects to result in the development of showers and thunderstorms across interior and western PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding reported in the urban sector of Rincon along Road PR-115 between Costa Salud Hospital and Econo Supermarket.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The available moisture combined with the diurnal heating and local effects to result in the development of showers and thunderstorms across interior, western, and southwest PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Berrenchin Creek flooded Road 335 in Barrio Barinas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak steering flow and normal moisture content combined to produce showers and heavy rainfall across the region. The rain over already saturated soils resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Video posted on social media showed a strong water current moving along PR-111 into Herrera Avenue near LaarCoop Credit Union.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak steering flow and normal moisture content combined to produce showers and heavy rainfall across the region. The rain over already saturated soils resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported near Esteves Ave. near Monserrate Moreno School due to Rio Vivi out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Plenty of low-level moisture combined with diurnal heating and local effects to result in the development of slow moving showers, mainly over interior PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding and mudslide prompted the closure of road 123 K.M. 55 in Sector Salto Arriba, Utuado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unstable pattern associated with a surface trough and an upper level trough produced periods of heavy rainfall across the local islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three rescues made for people on their stranded cars at Rio Turabo, roadway 765, k.m. 0.3 in Barrio Borinquen, Caguas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper-level jet, which produced divergence aloft, combined with sufficient low-level moisture and local and diurnal effects to produce strong showers and thunderstorms over the Cordillera Central.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail was reported in Barrio Cordillera along PR-146 near Escuela Superior Juan Antonio Corretjer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper-level trough along with abundant moisture resulted in the development of showers and thunderstorms across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding reported in Calle Texidor in Barrio Israel.
